blurb:
This is the answer when your group can't agree on where to eat. It's a food hall right off the Square with around twenty different vendors, so everyone gets to pick what they want. Works great for families with picky kids, lunch with coworkers, or just a Saturday afternoon walk around the Square.

Because the vendors rotate and cover a wide range of cuisines, it's hard to pin down one must-try dish, but the variety is genuinely the whole point. You can grab something different every time you go.

Hours run until 10pm on Fridays and Saturdays, which makes it a decent option for a casual evening out on the Square.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM

blurb:
This is my go-to when I want a solid, affordable lunch without a lot of fuss. It's on Franklin Gateway and easy to overlook, but the place has a loyal following for a reason. Good for a quick weekday lunch or an easy weeknight dinner when you don't feel like cooking.

The shawarma is what most people order, and it's the real deal. The falafel is great too, and there are solid veggie options if you have someone in your group who doesn't eat meat. Prices are very reasonable, which makes it even easier to come back.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, so the hours are pretty flexible.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
If you ever need a meal at 2am after a late night out, or just want a massive breakfast on a Sunday morning, this is the place. The neon sign on Cobb Pkwy is hard to miss, and it never closes. Seriously, 24 hours a day, every day of the week.

The menu is huge, and I mean huge. They do classic American diner food alongside Greek specialties, which is a fun combination. The big breakfasts are what a lot of people come for, and the portions are not shy.

Parking can get tight during weekend brunch hours, so just be ready for a short wait if you go then. Otherwise it's a pretty easy in-and-out.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is the place you go when you want to actually impress someone. It sits right along the Chattahoochee River off Powers Ferry, and the view alone is worth the trip. Good for a birthday dinner, an anniversary, or any night when you want the whole experience to feel special.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, so expect fresh fish and classic preparations done really well. It's on the pricier side, but most people feel like it's worth it for the occasion. The Sunday brunch is also popular if you want a slightly more relaxed version of the same setting.

Sunday hours are split, so check the schedule before you go. They close in the afternoon and reopen for dinner.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This place has a 4.9 rating from over 5,000 reviews and honestly, that tracks. It's traditional Italian thin-crust pizza done right, and it's the kind of spot that becomes a regular go-to pretty fast. Good for a casual date night or a Friday dinner when everyone just wants something really good without a lot of fuss.

The pizzas use ingredients sourced from Italy or locally, and you can taste the difference. The antipasti and salads are worth ordering alongside the pizza. It doesn't feel like a chain, it feels like someone who actually cares about the food runs the place.

They're open late on Fridays and Saturdays until 11pm, which is nice if you're not in a hurry.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you've never had Hyderabadi biryani, this is a solid place to start. It's colorful and casual inside, and the food comes out family-style, which makes it great for a group dinner or a night when everyone wants to try a little of everything. It's right off Perimeter Center, so easy to get to.

The biryani is the main event here, and most people come specifically for that. Traditional Indian dishes, nothing too fussy, and portions are generous. Good spot for a weeknight dinner when you want something hearty and different without spending a lot.

They open early on weekends at 9am and stay open late on Fridays and Saturdays, so it works for a late dinner too.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
I don't know how this place has nearly 16,000 reviews and a 4.9 rating, but after you go once you'll understand. It's a French bakery on South Marietta Pkwy and it feels like a little escape from the suburbs. Perfect for a slow weekend morning or a weekday treat you'll look forward to all day.

The baguettes and quiches are the real deal, and the petits fours make a great gift if you need to bring something to someone. Everything is made well and the space is bright and welcoming.

Just note that Sunday hours are shorter, running only from 9am to 3:30pm, so don't show up late expecting a leisurely brunch.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM - 3:30 PM

blurb:
This place is great when you want something a little different and you're hungry enough to make it worthwhile. It's an all-you-can-eat Brazilian spot on Powers Ferry, and the barbecue keeps coming until you tell them to stop. Good for a group dinner or a fun date night when you want to linger and eat way too much.

The cheese bread alone is worth the trip. The feijoada stew is a hearty, traditional Brazilian dish and a good thing to try if you haven't had it before. They also do breakfast, which is a fun surprise for a place like this.

They're open until 11pm on Fridays and Saturdays, so it works well as a later dinner spot.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM

blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places where the hype is actually real. The vibe inside is fun and loud in a good way, and the staff is genuinely helpful, which makes it easy for first-timers to figure out what to order. Great for a group night out or even just a weeknight when you want something lively.

Start with the Esquite and order a pitcher of the Pineapple Guava or the Passion Fruit. Both are really refreshing and not overly sweet. Then get the tacos, obviously. It's open until midnight most nights, so it works well as a late dinner option too.

Parking is in the Sandy Springs Place shopping center, so it's easy to get in and out.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Yalda is genuinely one of the best dinner spots in the area right now and a lot of people don't know about it yet. It's Persian food done in a way that feels both approachable and a little special, which makes it perfect for a girls' night out or a dinner with people you want to impress without going somewhere stuffy.

Order the hummus and wings to start. The chicken shish kabobs are the move for a main, especially with the rice with lentils and raisins on the side. Finish with the tiramisu. The cocktails are also worth trying, and the whole experience moves at a good pace.

Just note they close in the afternoon on weekdays between lunch and dinner, so plan around that if you're going during the week.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Mon-Thu 11:30am-2:45pm and 4pm-9:30pm, Fri 11:30am-2:45pm and 4pm-10:30pm, Sat 11am-2:45pm and 4pm-10:15pm, Sun 11am-2:45pm and 4pm-9:30pm
